description: Figure 2 presents electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) inverse pole figure (IPF) orientation maps comparing the grain structure of alloy M2 (a Ti/V-modified Fe–Mn–Al–C austenitic low-density steel) in the solution-treated state (a, M2-SS) and after aging at 550 °C for 30 h (b, M2-30). Both maps display equiaxed austenite grains with a seemingly random color distribution across the orientation triangle—visually consistent with the absence of strong crystallographic texture—and share an identical 50 μm scale bar; panel (b) includes the standard IPF color key for the [001]–[101]–[111] crystallographic directions. While the images directly confirm a recrystallized, equiaxed matrix in both conditions, related EBSD analysis reported in the text indicates that M2-SS exhibits a bimodal grain-size distribution, whereas M2-30 shows a unimodal distribution with modest grain coarsening during aging. These orientation maps provide essential microstructural context, demonstrating that Ti/V microalloying produces a fine-grained, randomly textured austenite matrix and that aging does not induce preferential grain orientation, thereby supporting the study’s central argument that precipitation of (Ti,V)C carbides and heterogeneous κ-carbides dictates the mechanical property evolution.
